> [!IMPORTANT]

## Root Context

\n# b1CodingTool: Global Context
This is the root `agent.md` file managed by b1CodingTool. 
It contains project-agnostic software development practices and general notes.

## General Guidelines
- Write clean, modular code.
- Include thoughtful documentation.

## Commands

```bash
uv run b1 --help                     # Show CLI help
uv run b1 init                       # Bootstrap a project with .agent/ structure
uv run b1 install <module-source> [--link] # Install a module (local path or git URL)
uv run b1 pull                       # Sync modules from upstream repo
```

## Architecture

```
src/b1/
  cli.py               # Typer app entry point — registers all commands
  commands/            # One file per CLI command (init, install, pull, push, pair, dashboard)
  core/
    compiler.py        # Compiles agent.md hierarchy into a single string
    translator.py      # Writes compiled string to CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, CODEX.md
    context_manager.py # Scaffolds agent.md files on b1 init
    installer.py       # Copies or symlinks (--link) module files into .agent/modules/
    fetcher.py         # Resolves module source: local path or git clone to ~/.b1/cache/
    scaffolder.py      # Scaffolds .agent/ directory structure
    schema.py          # Pydantic models: ModuleConfig, SkillConfig
    config.py          # Reads/writes .agent/config.yaml (B1Config model)
  server/main.py       # FastAPI server that serves data to the React dashboard
dashboard/             # React frontend (Vite + TypeScript)
docs/                  # Planning docs, SPEC.md, and MODULE_GUIDE.md
```

For detailed information on creating and structuring modules, refer to the **[Module Authoring Guide](docs/MODULE_GUIDE.md)**.

## Synergy: Public & Private Repositories
To maintain an open-source core while keeping your "secret sauce" private:
1. **Public Repo (`b1CodingTool`)**: Focus on baseline framework standards (e.g., `flutter`, `django`).
2. **Private Repo (`b1CodingTool-LLC-lib`)**: Focus on proprietary extensions prefixed with `llc-` (e.g., `llc-flutter`).
3. **Parallel Growth**: Use `b1 install <private-path> --link` to actively develop proprietary modules within a consumer project while keeping the private repo as the source of truth.

## Design Synergy
Always check for `llc-` modules in the private repository (`b1CodingTool-LLC-lib`) when building B1Codes software. These modules provide the "Liquid Glass" overrides and proprietary design tokens that elevate the baseline community standards to the B1Codes premium aesthetic.


## Gotchas
- CLAUDE.md, GEMINI.md, and CODEX.md are AUTO-GENERATED by `b1 pair` — edit `agent.md` or `.agent/project/agent.md` instead, then re-run `b1 pair`.
- Modules must contain `b1-module.yaml` (or `module.yaml`) at their root to be recognized.
- Remote module cache lives at `~/.b1/cache/` (git clones); re-install pulls latest.
- Config state stored at `.agent/config.yaml` — tracks `upstream_repo` and `active_agents`.
- `uv` is used for all Python dependency management and running — not pip or poetry.\n\n

## Project Context

\n# b1CodingTool: Project Context
This is the project-specific `agent.md` context file.
It contains app logic, directory structures, and active tasks.

## Active Tasks
- Build out the module registry with real modules

## Key Design Decisions
- `b1 pair` compiles the full agent.md hierarchy (root → project → module context files) into one string, then writes identical content to all agent-specific files. Per-agent formatting differences (e.g., XML tags for Claude) are a future enhancement in `translator.py`.
- Module install is intentionally non-destructive: if a skill's setup script fails, the error is logged but the rest of the installation completes.
- `ContextCompiler` assembles content in order: root `agent.md` → `.agent/project/agent.md` → each installed module's `context/*.md` files.

## Architecture Notes
- Follow the guidelines specified in the root `agent.md`.\n\n
